steps.title: Set up the angle relationship
steps.content: Lines $\ell$ and $k$ are parallel; transversal $t$ creates co-interior (same-side interior) angles $x$ and $y$. By the Co-Interior Angle Theorem these are supplementary: $$x + y = 180°$$

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'y < 15': Because $y = 180 - x$ and $x > 165$, we get $y \lt 15$. ✓
  • Choice B — 'y > 15': This requires $180 - x > 15$, i.e., $x \lt 165$. That contradicts $x > 165$. ✗
  • Choice C — 'x + y < 180': Co-interior angles on parallel lines sum to exactly 180, never less. ✗
  • Choice D — 'x + y > 180': Same reason; the sum is always exactly 180. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
